title: How Braintrust turns customer requests into code with Codex
optimized_headline: "Discover How Braintrust Transforms Customer Requests into Code Using Codex"
raw_excerpt: How Braintrust engineers use Codex with GPT-5.5 to run experiments and code faster.
raw_body: How Braintrust engineers use Codex with GPT-5.5 to run experiments and code faster.
speedrun: Braintrust engineers are leveraging Codex alongside GPT-5.5 to enhance their coding efficiency. By integrating these advanced AI tools, they can convert customer requests directly into code, streamlining the development process. This collaboration allows for faster experimentation and iteration. As technology evolves, the ability to quickly translate ideas into functional code could significantly impact software development timelines.
why_it_matters: For engineers, this means faster turnaround times on projects, enabling them to respond to customer needs more effectively.
On a larger scale, this reflects a shift in how software development teams might operate, emphasizing AI-assisted coding to improve productivity.

lenses.eli12: Braintrust is using smart AI tools, Codex and GPT-5.5, to help engineers write code more quickly based on what customers want. Think of it like having a personal assistant that turns your ideas into a finished product faster. This is important because it could make software more responsive to user needs, benefiting everyone who relies on technology.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this development highlights a growing user need for rapid iteration in software. By adopting AI tools like Codex, teams can reduce costs and improve efficiency in coding. This means faster updates and features, which could enhance user satisfaction and competitiveness.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, Braintrust's use of Codex with GPT-5.5 allows for more efficient coding by translating customer requests into code snippets. This integration could lead to reduced development cycles and improved accuracy in meeting user specifications. However, it's vital to monitor the performance and limitations of these AI tools in real-world applications.
